headline: Chip bull market showing cracks
detail: Traders are actively questioning whether the AI/chip rally has run its course and are looking for protection. This is the single biggest macro threat to your portfolio since you are roughly 90% concentrated in tech and chip names. On margin, a sector rotation or correction hits you harder than most.

headline: Broadcom eyes OpenAI Jalapeno chip deal
detail: Broadcom is reportedly co-designing a custom AI chip called Jalapeno with OpenAI, which could be a meaningful revenue catalyst. This matters directly for AVGO, your largest position, which is already down nearly 13%. A credible win here could stabilize it, but the stock needs confirmation, not just headlines.

headline: Nvidia CEO warns on smuggled chip black market
detail: Jensen Huang calling black-market AI data centers a dead end signals Nvidia is aware of and pushing back on demand leakage through smuggled chips. It reinforces Nvidia's dominance narrative, which is broadly positive for chip sentiment and indirectly supports names like SMH and AVGO in your book.

headline: Wall Street buy-the-dip consensus is a warning sign
detail: When everyone agrees on buying dips, contrarian risk rises sharply — crowded trades unwind fast and violently. Given your margin exposure and concentration in high-beta tech names, a sudden dip-buying failure could cause outsized damage to your portfolio before stops are triggered.
